protocol: Zero-shot only: no task-specific fine-tuning or frozen head is used.
The examples ask whether a driver will fail to finish, what qualifying position an entry will get, and what race finishing position an entry will get.

Complete official test tables are scored with the RelBench task metric. Both runners request 1,024-cell reference contexts, seed 0, BFS width 32, 10,000 walks, and walk length 20. Any OOM fallback is isolated to the failing model.

runners.xgboost: Reference-style SQL relational features with the same validation-tuned 200-tree XGBoost settings used by the main comparison.
runners.ours: RelativeDB native C++ RT-J on MPS, consuming the reference Rust sampler's exact tensors without task-specific fitting.

limitations: This is a small query-behavior experiment, not the 21-task benchmark.

The row-level finishing-position task exposes other completed-race fields, including official finish order, to both methods; it measures masked-column completion rather than forecasting before a race.
